candidates.title: "How It Works" pipeline explainer section
candidates.summary: Add a static, visual step-by-step explainer to the homepage showing the 5-stage autonomous pipeline: Explore, Score, Build, Test, Ship.

keep: Explains the system before visitors see empty states — sets the right expectation
Pure HTML/CSS means zero maintenance overhead
mustChange: Descriptions must stay high-level so they don't go stale as the pipeline evolves
risks: If pipeline stages change names, the section needs a manual update

keep: First-time visitors immediately understand what Command Garden does
Visual pipeline is screenshot-worthy for social media
mustChange: Ensure responsive layout doesn't break on narrow viewports
risks: Section could feel like marketing fluff if descriptions are too vague

keep: Makes the empty garden feel intentional rather than broken
Emoji icons give it personality without adding asset dependencies
mustChange: Add connector arrows between steps for desktop to reinforce the flow
risks: Low novelty — explainer sections are common, needs strong visual execution

keep: Explains the system before visitors see empty states
mustChange: Keep descriptions high-level to avoid staleness
Ensure responsive layout works on all viewports
risks: Pipeline stage names could change in the future
Explainer sections are common — needs strong visual execution
feedbackReferences: Multiple visitors were confused by empty states on the homepage

candidates.title: Add a 'Garden Status' live indicator
candidates.summary: Show a real-time status badge indicating which pipeline stage is currently running.
keep: Real-time status creates a reason to revisit
Highly novel — visitors can watch the AI work
mustChange: Requires a new API endpoint or polling mechanism — too complex for day 2
risks: Low feasibility — needs backend infrastructure that doesn't exist yet

candidates.title: Add a contributor credits section
candidates.summary: Display the AI models and tools involved in building the site, with links to their documentation.
keep: Transparency is a core value — credits reinforce that
mustChange: Needs to auto-update as the tool stack changes
risks: Low compounding value — static content that rarely changes

winner.title: "How It Works" pipeline explainer section
winner.summary: Add a static, visual step-by-step explainer to the homepage showing the 5-stage autonomous pipeline: Explore, Score, Build, Test, Ship.

rationale: The explainer section scored highest across all judges because it directly addresses the most pressing visitor confusion: seeing empty states on a brand-new site. By showing the 5-stage pipeline visually, visitors understand the system is intentionally just starting, not broken. Its pure HTML/CSS implementation makes it the most feasible candidate with zero ongoing maintenance. The visual pipeline is also screenshot-worthy, making it shareable on social media.

feedbackInfluence.summary: Visitors were confused by empty states on the homepage, not understanding the site had just launched.
